summary: This source is an award tribute summarizing Dr. Julie E. Buring's career in epidemiology. It highlights her leadership in major randomized clinical trials (such as the Women's Health Study and VITAL) and her advocacy for transparent doctor-patient communication regarding complementary and integrative medicine.

principles.statement: Physicians must proactively ask patients about their use of complementary and integrative health treatments.
principles.rationale: Many patients use alternative treatments but do not disclose them simply because their doctor never asked. Proactive communication prevents missed opportunities to safely advise on proven treatments.
principles.source_quote: Dr burring argued that this lack of communication results in missed opportunities for Physicians to safely advise on proven complementary and Integrative Health treatments

anti_patterns.description: Physicians waiting for patients to voluntarily disclose their use of complementary and integrative health treatments.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Patients often will not bring it up unless explicitly asked, leading to an information gap that prevents doctors from providing safe, evidence-based guidance on these treatments.
